notes: Pi natively reopens a prior session at relaunch (`pi -c` continue-last / `pi -r` / `pi --session <uuid>`), reusing the SAME uuid.jsonl under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/--<cwd>--/. Because the adapter watches that directory (FilesUnderRoot), the resumed process appends to the existing transcript and the daemon sees the SAME session_id row come back after the first lifetime exits — exactly the claudecode shape. Target arc: session S born ready, runs one turn, the process exits, then the SAME uuid resumes (a second process binds, the same session_id reappears in ready), and a second turn drives ready→working→ready on that same session. This expected.jsonl is the documented TARGET — it validates once the pi interactive driver gains exit_clean + resume step types (drive-pi-interactive.sh currently lacks both; cell is recorded applicable:false until then). Variant 2 (multiple kill+resume across PID lifetimes) shares this observability surface and can be a follow-up cell.

text: First launch: session appears in ready
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Spec: 'Same session_id reappears (not a new session row)' — the resumed pi reopens the same uuid.jsonl, so the daemon observes the SAME session_id come back in ready under a new PID
Spec: 'ready → working on the first new turn' of the resumed lifetime
Second turn completes back to ready (each PID lifetime's arc is independently correct on the same session row)
